steps.title: Identify the height-radius constraint and volume formula
steps.content: The problem tells us the height is 4 inches longer than the radius, so $h = r + 4$. The volume of a cylinder is $V = \pi r^2 h$.

steps.title: Substitute and expand
steps.content:
Substitute $h = r + 4$ into the volume formula:

$$V = \pi r^2(r + 4)$$

Distributing $\pi r^2$ gives:

$$V = \pi r^3 + 4\pi r^2$$

This matches choice D — D is correct.
